fresh_facts.claim: A third U.S. aircraft carrier is en route to the region near the Strait of Hormuz alongside logistical reinforcements and military drills.
The U.S. maintains a naval blockade on Iranian ports with recent seizure of an Iranian-bound cargo vessel, escalating maritime tensions.
President Trump threatened strikes on Iranian infrastructure if negotiations fail, warning of 'problems like they have never had before.'
An Iranian cargo ship 'Shoja 2' crossed the Hormuz Strait despite the U.S. blockade, asserting Iranian naval dominance.
Iran is coordinating with militias in Iraq for potential joint attacks if fighting resumes, according to IRGC assessments.
A violent internal power struggle at the top of the IRGC threatens regime stability, with unusual confrontations and mutual accusations among senior Tehran officials.
IRGC Commander Ahmed Vahidi insists on a firm negotiating stance amid debates over arming proxies versus aid and ballistic missiles.
Iran released a video on April 19 showing active underground missile and drone facilities under IRGC-linked overhaul.
High-level Iran-Pakistan calls failed to advance talks, with Iran citing U.S. ship seizures as obstacles to negotiations.
A senior U.S. delegation heads to Pakistan for mediation, with VP Vance potentially joining on April 22.
Trump signals low chance of extending the ceasefire deadline while expressing deal confidence under strict conditions.
China criticizes U.S. actions in the region and may intervene diplomatically.
U.S. anti-war protests saw over 60 veterans detained amid 415 reported injuries domestically.
U.S. strikes in Operation Midnight Hammer hit three Iranian nuclear facilities.
Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and senior Iranian officials were killed in U.S.-Israel strikes on February 28, 2026.
An Interim Leadership Council composed of President Masoud Pezeshkian, Chief Justice Gholam-Hossein Mohseni-Eje'i, and Guardian Council representative Alireza Arafi holds temporary power.
The Assembly of Experts seeks a new Supreme Leader with no clear timeline or frontrunner established.
Iran rejects U.S. talks 'under the shadow of threats,' warning of 'new cards' and military options if no agreement precedes ceasefire expiry.
Iran affirms attendance at Pakistan-hosted second-round talks despite Strait of Hormuz hostilities.
Trump extended the ceasefire at Pakistan's request for Iran to unify its position before second-round talks.
Trump insists on ending Iran's nuclear program in a deal 'better than JCPOA' with no further extension extension 'highly unlikely.'
The regime confronts a structural legitimacy crisis from strikes, crackdowns detaining tens of thousands, and disputed casualties.
Iran admits approximately 3,000 deaths from strikes, while rights groups claim multiples higher.
Analysts question if this is Iran's 'Gorbachev moment' amid petrochemical shortages from hub strikes and hardliner rise post-Khamenei.
Recent protests represent Iran's gravest domestic threat ever, risking regime character change amid crackdowns and external vulnerabilities.
Deadly unrest occurred in Mashhad with network blackouts reported.
Strikes caused severe petrochemical shortages in Iran.
Hormuz disruptions, blockades, and scam crypto demands for safe passage exacerbate Iranian instability.
Global markets react to Iran tensions with gold above $4,800 and USD/CHF near 0.7800.
The U.S. embassy warns of Iran-linked militia threats in Iraq and Kurdistan.

regime_fall.current_status: Iran faces unprecedented simultaneous pressures: three U.S. carriers and naval blockade intensify military encirclement, an interim leadership council governs following Khamenei's death with no clear Supreme Leader successor, internal IRGC power struggles threaten cohesion, massive domestic protests and crackdowns signal legitimacy crisis, and ceasefire talks remain deadlocked with Iran rejecting U.S. terms while economy deteriorates from strikes and petrochemical shortages.
regime_fall.last_major_event: An Interim Leadership Council (President Pezeshkian, Chief Justice Mohseni-Eje'i, Guardian Council's Arafi) assumed temporary power following Supreme Leader Khamenei's death in U.S.-Israel strikes on February 28, with the Assembly of Experts seeking a new Supreme Leader amid ongoing external military pressure and internal power struggles.
